name: penumbra-glsl
description: Penumbra-in-Rhino IN-PROCESS GLSL studio suite (Swath C+). Drives CPig live preview through the native GLSL conduit (PenumbraPreviewBackend Glsl). Two tests cover both render paths + form a shape-discrimination pair: penumbra-glsl-00-sphere (a Field Primitive sphere → STRUCTURAL tape path) + penumbra-glsl-01-cpig-gyroid (unified Field TPMS gyroid∩sphere → DENSE voxel-bake/grid path, Penumbra bug 0054). PREVIEW DISCIPLINE: the Slop fixtures set preview:false on every field-producing node, so ONLY the Penumbra render appears in the viewport — a visible GH field preview (the CPig Field* components draw a bbox+iso-mesh) clutters/occludes the SDF and masks whether Penumbra rendered at all. ASSERTS: deterministic panel asserts (SlopSuccess / Connected / Status=Pushed / no FATAL) prove the CPig→Penumbra push + connection regardless of framing. VISUAL: mode:capture — saves the candidate for manual review (inspect it + the gl.scene.loaded telemetry); no pass/fail on the image. VLM regression IS wired and dormant (each test carries setup.vlm = ollama qwen2.5vl:7b — a local vision model, no API key — + a discriminating vlmDescription); re-enable by setting the checkpoint mode back to "vlm". HOW TO RUN: `canary run --workload rhino --suite penumbra-glsl`. Runs SHARED — both tests chain in ONE Rhino (runMode shared, now the default); WaitForPenumbraFrame is RELATIVE (waits for a NEW frame past a baseline snapshotted at action start), so the process-global GLSL frame-ready revision can't cause a stale-frame capture across chained tests. Requires the Penumbra Rhino plug-in registered for auto-load + the CPig .gha (Penumbra Preview). DIAGNOSTIC: if the viewport is empty (no Penumbra render), the SDF most likely rendered unframed/tiny (pushed-scene auto-fit = roadmap A2) or the device-free node host is stale (restart Rhino) — the sphere test (tape path) vs the gyroid test (dense-grid path) isolates which render path is at fault.
